title: oxalis glauca
intro: Oxalis glauca, also known as purple wood sorrel, is a perennial plant native to the Americas, characterized by its purple flowers and sour-tasting leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Oxalis glauca include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve digestion, and even help manage certain types of cancer. The plant is often prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including urinary tract infections and skin issues.
